promotionPolicy.latencyPolicy: RPC latency remains report-only in bench/budget-ratchet.json until trend confidence is promoted
promotionPolicy.allocationPolicy: blocking when bench-regression-check budget covers RPC hot paths

budgetEvidence.currentPolicy: rpcPolicy.status remains report-only until RPC hot-path allocation and latency rows meet promotion criteria

remainingBlockers.requiredAction: attach at least one stable release train with complete rpc-boundary and bench-evidence artifacts before Tier 1 promotion
promote selected RPC unary or stream rows from rpcPolicy.candidates into blocking budget coverage only after trend confidence passes

r8StreamingBoundaryMatrix.summary: R8 keeps Kitex and gRPC-Go transport depth out of scope while making gofly unary, streaming, resolver, balancer, retry, deadline, auth, tracing, benchmark, and rollback evidence machine-checkable.

rows.runtimeCompatibility: gofly unary RPC is usable for governed service glue after unary trend, error-code mapping, and retry evidence passes.
rows.rollbackTrigger: Route latency-critical unary calls back to Kitex or native gRPC-Go when unary allocation, latency, or retry evidence is missing or regresses.

rows.runtimeCompatibility: Server-stream adoption requires mode-specific governance overhead, auth, tracing, and compatibility evidence before replacing the existing stream path.
rows.rollbackTrigger: Keep server-stream workloads on Kitex or gRPC-Go when stream overhead exceeds report-only budget or metadata propagation differs.

rows.runtimeCompatibility: Client-stream adoption requires upload-style stream governance, auth, tracing, and generated IDL matrix evidence.
rows.rollbackTrigger: Keep upload streams on the existing RPC stack when client-stream benchmark, auth, tracing, or generated IDL evidence is incomplete.

rows.runtimeCompatibility: Bidirectional streaming stays candidate-only until stream governance, metadata propagation, and rollback evidence are stable for the adopter workload.
rows.rollbackTrigger: Keep realtime bidirectional sessions on Kitex or native gRPC-Go when stream budget or compatibility evidence is missing.

rows.runtimeCompatibility: Resolver and balancer adoption requires discovery update, health-aware routing, and retry decision evidence for the target adapter.
rows.rollbackTrigger: Keep service mesh routing or the previous client balancer when resolver updates, endpoint health, or routing policy evidence diverges.

rows.runtimeCompatibility: rpc/grpc compatibility evidence is usable when deadline mapping, retryable statuses, auth failures, health, and trace metadata stay stable.
rows.rollbackTrigger: Keep native gRPC-Go, Kitex, or go-zero behavior when deadline taxonomy, retry policy, auth metadata, or trace propagation differs from production samples.

rows.runtimeCompatibility: Framework coexistence requires generated upgrade evidence, control-plane visibility, discovery registration, and explicit rollback routing before traffic migration.
rows.rollbackTrigger: Keep the original Kitex or go-zero service registered and route traffic back when generated output, control-plane, discovery, or RPC evidence diverges.

adopterContract.policy: Adopters may use gofly RPC surfaces only behind the listed boundary, benchmark evidence, and rollback gates while Tier 1 promotion stays blocked by release-train and budget evidence.

tierPolicy.releaseTrainRequirement: At least one completed release train must attach release evidence and required-check drift evidence before Tier 1 promotion.
tierPolicy.budgetRequirement: RPC hot-path benchmarks remain report-only until selected unary or stream rows enter bench-regression-check blocking budget coverage.

surfaceClasses.adopterDecision: Use rpc/grpc where health, auth, tracing, governance interceptors, deadline mapping, and retry semantics pass local compatibility gates.
surfaceClasses.rollbackAction: Keep native gRPC-Go clients and servers reachable until compatibility tests and release evidence pass for the adopter workload.
surfaceClasses.supportBundleAction: Attach gofly bug --json with rpc/grpc health, auth, tracing, governance interceptor, deadline mapping, and retry evidence before escalation.
surfaceClasses.failureReportEvidence: Capture gRPC status code, health SERVING state, auth metadata, traceparent propagation, timeout policy, and retry decision drift.

surfaceClasses.adopterDecision: Use gofly RPC for governed service glue only after unary, stream, resolver, and balancer evidence passes in the target repository.
surfaceClasses.rollbackAction: Route hot or latency-sensitive methods back to Kitex or the existing gRPC path when benchmark evidence is missing or regresses.
surfaceClasses.supportBundleAction: Attach gofly bug --json with unary, stream, resolver, balancer, governance, and benchmark evidence before moving governed RPC into production.
surfaceClasses.failureReportEvidence: Capture unary result drift, stream mode drift, resolver update drift, balancer routing drift, governance policy output, and benchmark regression evidence.

surfaceClasses.adopterDecision: Treat RPC benchmark rows as trend evidence until bench-regression-check promotes selected candidates into blocking budget coverage.
surfaceClasses.rollbackAction: Block Tier 1 promotion and keep latency-critical traffic on Kitex or existing gRPC clients while budgets remain report-only.
surfaceClasses.supportBundleAction: Attach gofly bug --json with bench evidence, budget-ratchet status, release-train status, and Kitex rollback context for performance escalations.
surfaceClasses.failureReportEvidence: Capture benchmark name, sample count, allocs/op, latency trend, ratchet classification, and rollback decision before promoting RPC budgets.

surfaceClasses.adopterDecision: Plan migration with parallel registration, rollback routing, and generated upgrade dry-run evidence before cutting traffic over.
surfaceClasses.rollbackAction: Keep the original Kitex or go-zero service registered and route traffic back when generated output, control-plane, or RPC evidence diverges.
surfaceClasses.supportBundleAction: Attach gofly bug --json with coexistence routing, generated dry-run, discovery registration, control-plane, and rollback evidence.
surfaceClasses.failureReportEvidence: Capture generated output drift, discovery registration drift, control-plane mismatch, routing decision, and rollback trigger for coexistence failures.

promotionDecision.reason: RPC has boundary, stream, resolver, balancer, deadline, retry, auth, tracing, and rollback evidence, but no completed release train and no blocking RPC benchmark budget coverage yet.
promotionDecision.latencyPolicy: RPC latency remains report-only; release notes must not claim Kitex, gRPC-Go, Netpoll, TTHeader, or Thrift transport parity.
promotionDecision.nextEligibleDecision: Re-evaluate after one release train attaches release evidence, required-check drift evidence, rpc-boundary-check, bench-evidence-check, and bench-regression-check outputs.

releaseTrainRequirements.promotionUse: Proves the release train that would carry Tier 1 RPC evidence is auditable by artifact, required-check, and release evidence.
Keeps unary, stream, resolver, balancer, deadline, retry, auth, tracing, and rollback boundaries machine-verifiable.
Blocks Tier 1 promotion until selected RPC unary or stream rows gain blocking allocation budget coverage and latency remains explicitly report-only or separately promoted.

surfaceCloseout.blockingGap: BenchmarkRPCUnary/gofly_rpc remains outside trackedBenchmarks while allocation and latency promotion criteria are incomplete.
surfaceCloseout.rollbackAction: Keep latency-critical unary methods on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until RPC unary budgets become blocking.

surfaceCloseout.blockingGap: Server stream governance evidence exists, but five-sample baseline and current trend rows are not promoted into blocking budget coverage.
surfaceCloseout.rollbackAction: Keep server-stream workloads on the existing gRPC or Kitex path until mode-specific budget evidence is promoted.

surfaceCloseout.blockingGap: Client stream governance evidence exists, but upload-style stream budgets remain report-only.
surfaceCloseout.rollbackAction: Keep upload streams on the previous RPC stack until client-stream benchmark evidence becomes blocking.

surfaceCloseout.blockingGap: Bidirectional stream governance evidence exists, but realtime workload budgets remain report-only.
surfaceCloseout.rollbackAction: Keep realtime bidirectional sessions on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until promoted budget evidence exists.

surfaceCloseout.blockingGap: Resolver and balancer smoke exists, but target adapter rollout still requires local discovery evidence before traffic migration.
surfaceCloseout.rollbackAction: Keep service mesh or previous client routing active until resolver updates and health-aware routing pass for the adopter environment.

surfaceCloseout.blockingGap: Compatibility evidence is ready, but Tier 1 promotion still waits on release train and RPC benchmark budget evidence.
surfaceCloseout.rollbackAction: Keep native gRPC-Go endpoints reachable until the release train proves compatibility and budget evidence together.

surfaceCloseout.blockingGap: Coexistence and rollback must remain mandatory because gofly does not claim Kitex or go-zero transport/runtime parity.
surfaceCloseout.rollbackAction: Keep original Kitex or go-zero services registered and route traffic back when generated output, control-plane, discovery, or RPC evidence diverges.

decision.reason: gRPC compatibility, stream governance, deadline/error-code, retry/balancer, and coexistence evidence are machine-checkable, but release train evidence and blocking RPC benchmark budgets are still incomplete.
decision.releaseNotePolicy: Do not claim Kitex, gRPC-Go, Netpoll, TTHeader, Thrift, or blocking RPC latency parity until the P10 closeout prerequisites are satisfied.

closeoutRows.promotionGap: Compatibility is ready for adopter review, but Tier 1 promotion still requires release train and benchmark budget evidence.
closeoutRows.rollbackAction: Keep native gRPC-Go endpoints reachable when health, auth, tracing, retry, or deadline semantics drift.

closeoutRows.promotionGap: Streaming governance evidence exists, but mode-specific stream baselines are not promoted into blocking budget coverage.
closeoutRows.rollbackAction: Keep server, client, or bidirectional streams on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until mode-specific evidence is promoted.

closeoutRows.promotionGap: Deadline and error-code mappings are stable evidence, but replacement decisions still require sampled adopter workload validation.
closeoutRows.rollbackAction: Keep existing gRPC-Go, Kitex, or go-zero error taxonomy when sampled production behavior requires a different mapping.

closeoutRows.promotionGap: Retry and balancer behavior is rehearsed, but target discovery adapter evidence must pass before traffic migration.
closeoutRows.rollbackAction: Keep service mesh or previous client-side routing active when retry status lists, endpoint health, or balancer policy evidence diverges.

closeoutRows.promotionGap: Coexistence remains mandatory because gofly does not claim Kitex or go-zero transport/runtime parity.
closeoutRows.rollbackAction: Keep original Kitex or go-zero services registered and route traffic back when generated output, control-plane, discovery, or RPC evidence diverges.

decision.reason: P11 promotion review confirms rpc/grpc compatibility and governed RPC evidence are useful for adopters, but Tier 1 promotion still lacks an attached release train and blocking RPC benchmark budget coverage.
decision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes must keep RPC latency parity, Kitex transport parity, gRPC-Go ecosystem parity, Netpoll, TTHeader, and Thrift replacement claims forbidden until P11 blockers are cleared.

reviewRows.promotionFinding: Unary evidence is usable for governed service glue, but the benchmark row remains outside blocking tracked budget coverage.
reviewRows.requiredAction: Attach release-train evidence and promote the selected unary benchmark budget before advertising Tier 1 replacement readiness.
reviewRows.rollbackAction: Keep latency-critical unary methods on Kitex or native gRPC-Go while unary allocation and latency evidence remains report-only.

reviewRows.promotionFinding: Server-stream governance evidence exists, but mode-specific release-train and budget evidence is still candidate-only.
reviewRows.requiredAction: Publish server-stream trend evidence and promote the chosen stream budget only after release-train validation passes.
reviewRows.rollbackAction: Keep server-stream workloads on Kitex or native gRPC-Go when metadata propagation, auth, tracing, or overhead evidence diverges.

reviewRows.promotionFinding: Client-stream governance is covered by smoke and compatibility evidence, but upload-style stream budgets remain report-only.
reviewRows.requiredAction: Attach current and baseline client-stream evidence before moving upload-style flows into blocking budget coverage.
reviewRows.rollbackAction: Keep upload streams on the previous RPC stack when client-stream benchmark, auth, tracing, or generated IDL evidence is incomplete.

reviewRows.promotionFinding: Bidirectional stream governance remains candidate evidence because realtime workload budgets are not yet blocking.
reviewRows.requiredAction: Publish bidirectional stream baseline and current trend evidence before any realtime Tier 1 promotion decision.
reviewRows.rollbackAction: Keep realtime bidirectional sessions on Kitex or native gRPC-Go while stream budget evidence is incomplete or regresses.

reviewRows.promotionFinding: Resolver and balancer contracts are rehearsed, but target discovery adapter evidence must still pass for each rollout.
reviewRows.requiredAction: Require adapter-specific resolver update, endpoint health, retry, and routing evidence before shifting production traffic.
reviewRows.rollbackAction: Keep service mesh routing or the previous client balancer active when resolver updates or health-aware routing diverges.

reviewRows.promotionFinding: gRPC compatibility is ready evidence for health, auth, tracing, deadline, and retry behavior, but it does not by itself satisfy release promotion.
reviewRows.requiredAction: Keep compatibility evidence attached to one complete release train before promoting rpc/grpc from Tier 2 to Tier 1.
reviewRows.rollbackAction: Keep native gRPC-Go endpoints reachable when health, auth, tracing, retry, or deadline semantics drift.

reviewRows.promotionFinding: Framework coexistence remains a release-blocking boundary because gofly does not claim Kitex or go-zero transport/runtime parity.
reviewRows.requiredAction: Keep rollback routing, generated upgrade dry-run evidence, and control-plane comparison mandatory for each migration.
reviewRows.rollbackAction: Keep original Kitex or go-zero services registered and route traffic back when generated output, control-plane, discovery, or RPC evidence diverges.

promotionBlockers.clearanceCondition: At least one release train must attach RPC boundary, required-check drift, release evidence, and benchmark evidence before promotion.
Selected RPC unary or stream rows must move from report-only candidates into blocking allocation budget coverage before promotion.
Documentation and release notes must continue to forbid Kitex, gRPC-Go, Netpoll, TTHeader, Thrift, and generic transport parity claims.

decision.reason: P12 keeps RPC unary and stream benchmark rows as report-only candidate evidence because no row currently satisfies release-train, multi-run baseline, current trend, allocation, and rollback criteria together.
decision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes must not claim RPC Tier 1 replacement readiness, blocking RPC latency, Kitex transport parity, or gRPC-Go ecosystem parity until a selected RPC row is promoted through the benchmark ratchet.

promotionPath.requiredEvidence: Choose exactly one unary or stream surface and keep all other RPC rows report-only.
promotionPath.rollbackAction: Keep all RPC workloads on their existing Kitex, native gRPC-Go, or previous RPC stack while no surface is selected.

promotionPath.requiredEvidence: Attach at least five baseline samples, three current trend samples, allocation budget evidence, and explicit latency report-only status before promotion.
promotionPath.rollbackAction: Do not add the RPC benchmark to trackedBenchmarks when any trend, allocation, or latency evidence is missing.

promotionPath.requiredEvidence: Attach one release train with RPC boundary, required-check drift, release evidence, benchmark evidence, and rollback guidance.
promotionPath.rollbackAction: Keep the Tier 1 promotion blocked when release evidence or required-check drift evidence is absent.

candidateSurfaces.rollbackAction: Keep latency-critical unary methods on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until unary allocation evidence is stable enough for blocking promotion.
Keep stream workloads on existing gRPC or Kitex paths until mode-specific stream budgets are promoted.
Keep server-stream workloads on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until server-stream allocation evidence is stable.
Keep upload-style client streams on the previous RPC stack until client-stream budget evidence is promoted.
Keep realtime bidirectional sessions on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until bidirectional stream evidence is promoted.

releaseTrainEvidence.clearanceCondition: A release train may clear this blocker only when RPC boundary evidence, benchmark ratchet evidence, release evidence, required-check drift, and rollback notes are attached together.

promotionDecision.reason: P13 keeps all RPC unary and stream surfaces outside allocation-blocking budgets because no surface has one complete release train plus five baseline samples, three current trend samples, no allocation regression, and rollback evidence together.
promotionDecision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes must describe RPC Tier 1 as blocked candidate evidence and must not claim Kitex transport parity, gRPC-Go ecosystem parity, blocking RPC latency, or drop-in replacement readiness.

stableSurfaceCriteria: exactly one RPC surface may be selected for allocation-blocking promotion at a time
selected RPC surface must have minimum 5 baseline samples
selected RPC surface must have minimum 3 current trend samples
selected RPC surface must pass bench-regression-check with no allocation regression
selected RPC surface latency remains report-only until maxRegressionRatio and two-release trend evidence are documented
selected RPC surface must keep a rollback action naming Kitex, native gRPC-Go, or the previous RPC stack

blockers: release train evidence is not attached
latest allocation evidence is not stable enough for blocking promotion
latency remains report-only without two-release trend confidence
surfaceMatrix.rollbackAction: Keep latency-critical unary methods on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until unary allocation evidence is stable enough for blocking promotion.

blockers: release train evidence is not attached
five-sample baseline is not published for server streaming
three-sample current trend is not published for server streaming
surfaceMatrix.rollbackAction: Keep server-stream workloads on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until server-stream allocation evidence is stable.

blockers: release train evidence is not attached
five-sample baseline is not published for upload-style streams
three-sample current trend is not published for upload-style streams
surfaceMatrix.rollbackAction: Keep upload-style client streams on the previous RPC stack until client-stream budget evidence is promoted.

blockers: release train evidence is not attached
five-sample baseline is not published for realtime bidirectional streams
three-sample current trend is not published for realtime bidirectional streams
surfaceMatrix.rollbackAction: Keep realtime bidirectional sessions on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until bidirectional stream evidence is promoted.

blockers: release train evidence is not attached
adapter-specific discovery matrix must pass for each production migration
health-aware rollback routing must stay attached
surfaceMatrix.rollbackAction: Keep service mesh routing or the previous client balancer active when resolver updates or health-aware routing diverges.

blockers: release train evidence is not attached
ready compatibility evidence must be attached to the selected RPC release train
surfaceMatrix.rollbackAction: Keep native gRPC-Go endpoints reachable when health, auth, tracing, retry, or deadline semantics drift.

blockingEvidence: release evidence manifest row for the selected RPC surface
required-check drift row proving the RPC gates are release prerequisites
bench-regression-check report with five baseline samples and three current trend samples
rollback note naming Kitex, native gRPC-Go, or the previous RPC stack
operator-facing release note that keeps latency report-only
releaseTrainEvidence.clearanceCondition: P14 may select at most one RPC surface only after release evidence, required-check drift, benchmark trend, allocation budget, and rollback evidence are attached together.

promotionDecision.reason: P14 keeps RPC unary and stream surfaces out of allocation-blocking budgets because no release train has attached complete RPC boundary, benchmark trend, required-check drift, release evidence, and rollback evidence together.
promotionDecision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes may describe RPC release-train evidence as blocked candidate evidence only and must not claim Kitex transport parity, gRPC-Go ecosystem parity, blocking RPC latency, drop-in RPC replacement, or Tier 1 promoted RPC readiness.

reviewRows.rollbackAction: Keep latency-critical unary methods on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until unary release-train and allocation evidence are complete.
Keep server-stream workloads on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until server-stream release-train and allocation evidence are complete.
Keep upload-style client streams on the previous RPC stack until client-stream release-train evidence is complete.
Keep realtime bidirectional sessions on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until bidirectional stream release-train evidence is complete.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Use gofly unary RPC for governed service glue only after gofly and gRPC-Go trend evidence is attached.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep latency-critical unary methods on existing Kitex or gRPC clients until RPC unary budgets become blocking.

contains: gofly RPC vs gRPC-Go benchmark evidence

evidence.decisionBoundary: Use gofly server streaming when governance metadata and mode-specific stream overhead are acceptable for the workload.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Route server streaming traffic back to the prior Kitex or gRPC stream path if governance overhead exceeds the report-only budget.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Use gofly client streaming when upload-style flows need policy evidence more than transport-layer specialization.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep client upload streams on the existing RPC stack until client-stream benchmark trend evidence is published.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Use gofly bidirectional streaming only for governed realtime flows that pass stream contract and rollback evidence.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep latency-sensitive bidirectional sessions on Kitex or gRPC until bidi stream budgets are promoted from report-only.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Use gofly discovery when resolver update tests prove unhealthy or standby endpoints are removed before routing.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin static endpoints or the previous discovery adapter until resolver update smoke passes.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Use gofly balancing when the chosen policy has deterministic routing evidence for the target migration path.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the upstream service mesh or existing client balancer until round-robin, weighted, P2C, hash, and health-aware smoke pass.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Keep Kitex on latency-critical transport paths while gofly owns governance, REST ingress, and generated control-plane surfaces.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Route hot methods back to Kitex when benchmark or stream contract evidence is missing or regresses.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Use rpc/grpc where ecosystem compatibility, health, auth, tracing, and governance interceptors are required.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep native gRPC-Go clients and servers active until health, auth, tracing, and governance compatibility tests pass.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Use gofly RPC or rpc/grpc only when deadline, timeout, validation, auth, and unavailable errors map to stable runtime codes.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the existing gRPC-Go, Kitex, or go-zero path active when mapped error codes drift from sampled production behavior.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Use gofly retry and balancing only when retryable statuses, resolver updates, and routing policy behavior match the migration plan.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep service-mesh or previous client-side routing in control when retry status lists, endpoint health, or balancer policy evidence is missing.

evidence.decisionBoundary: Migrate go-zero generated services only when REST/RPC shape, governance policy, discovery state, and rollback routing are all rehearsed.
evidence.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the go-zero service registered during rollout and route traffic back when generated output, control-plane, or RPC evidence diverges.

contains: Keep the previous go-zero service reachable
